body_txt: She loves him. He loves her, but they can never marry , because she's a princess , and he's not a suitable match for a princess. Maybe they're hiding it from each other, because "I can't allow myself to love someone like him"/"A princess could never love a guy like me". Maybe she'd like to run off with him, but can't because her people need her . Or maybe he just can't afford marriage — at least in the eyes of her social circle. For whatever reason, their marriage is forbidden — not by her parents, but by law or the rules of society. One or both parties may decide they will sacrifice the relationship to make their beloved happy . Then, his real parentage is discovered, and he's really a prince ; they may even have a Perfectly Arranged Marriage . If the issue preventing their marriage isn't that he isn't a prince, it's resolved, whatever it is: he may inherit enough to pay the Bride Price, or his Secret Legacy is revealed and he is now considered an acceptable spouse. If society would not accept it, the characters may resolve to defy it — and then get this as an added bonus . A common subversion is for the king to set aside the rule , allowing the couple to marry after all without the suitor becoming suitable. Another is for the princess to decide to ignore the social rules and marry the unsuitable suitor anyway. Differs from Parental Marriage Veto in that the rule is bigger than the parents, and the suitor becomes suitable not by convincing the parents but by social or financial improvement. If the way that the suitor becomes suitable involves heroic feats, it can overlap with Standard Hero Reward . It can also overlap with Engagement Challenge if the way to become a suitable suitor is by passing one. Expect spoilers afoot!
